title: Rosa canina var. innocua
what_description: Rosa canina var. innocua is a botanical variety of the dog rose species belonging to the family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous shrub with thorny stems, typically 2-5 meters in height, and ovate to lanceolate leaves that are 4-10 centimeters long and have 3-5 pairs of leaflets. The leaves are usually dark green on the upper surface and pale green on the underside, with a serrated margin. The flowers are fragrant, solitary, and 4-6 centimeters in diameter, with 5 petals and a cluster of stamens. Rosa canina var. innocua is native to Europe and has been introduced to other parts of the world as an ornamental plant.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Rosa canina var. innocua are diarrhea, which is alleviated by its antidiarrheal properties, skin irritation is reduced due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and inflammation is also alleviated by these properties. Inflammation is further reduced in wounds, which are also healed more quickly by the plant's antimicrobial properties. Diarrhea and digestive issues are also addressed through the plant's prebiotic properties, and fever is lowered by its antipyretic effects. Rosa canina var. innocua also helps with urinary tract infections due to its antimicrobial properties, and gastritis is alleviated by its anti-inflammatory effects, while sore throat is soothed by its expectorant properties. Furthermore, the plant helps with fungal infections, gastrointestinal issues, coughs, ulcers, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, and it accelerates wound healing and reduces the severity of cold sores.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Rosa canina var. innocua are its antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, due to the saponins and triterpenoids that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, Rosa canina var. innocua possesses antimicrobial properties, as a result of its volatile oils and saponins that disrupt microbial membranes and inhibit the growth of microorganisms. Furthermore, this plant has cardioprotective effects, which are attributed to the flavonoids and phenolic acids that reduce lipid peroxidation and improve cardiovascular function. The wound-healing properties of Rosa canina var. innocua are also noteworthy, as it promotes tissue repair and regeneration through the stimulation of collagen synthesis and the enhancement of antioxidant defenses.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Rosa canina var. innocua are flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Quercetin, a key flavonoid, is known for its ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit inflammatory enzymes, contributing to its anti-arthritic and anti-cancer effects. Gallic acid and ellagic acid, present in Rosa canina var. innocua, have been shown to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-proliferative activities, inhibiting cancer cell growth and inducing apoptosis in various cancer cell lines. Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic diterpene, is a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent that has been found to inhibit inflammatory cytokines and oxidative stress, contributing to its anti-arthritic and anti-asthmatic effects. Kaempferol, another flavonoid present in Rosa canina var. innocua, has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, inhibiting inflammatory enzymes and inducing apoptosis in cancer cells.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Rosa canina var. innocua are rosehip oil, rosehip tea, rosehip jam, rosehip syrup, and rosehip tincture. Rosehip oil is extracted from the fruit's seeds and is rich in essential fatty acids, vitamin C, and antioxidants, making it beneficial for skin and hair care. Rosehip tea is prepared by infusing the dried fruit in hot water and is used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. Rosehip jam and syrup are used as dietary supplements to provide essential nutrients, particularly vitamin C, and have been traditionally used to treat scurvy and other conditions. Rosehip tincture is a concentrated liquid extract made from the fruit's seeds and is used as a supplement to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Rosa canina var. innocua improperly include developing skin irritation due to allergic contact dermatitis caused by the allergenic compounds in the plant's sap, experiencing nausea or vomiting as a result of gastrointestinal upset from consuming excessive amounts of the plant's berries, causing allergic reactions in individuals with pre-existing allergies to the plant's sap or pollen, suffering kidney damage from long-term consumption of high doses of the plant's toxic compounds, and experiencing allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis, hives, and itching due to the plant's allergenic properties. This is because Rosa canina var. innocua contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Prolonged consumption of the plant's berries can also lead to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the plant's toxic compounds can cause kidney damage when consumed in large quantities over an extended period. It is essential to use Rosa canina var. innocua under the guidance of a healthcare professional to minimize the risk of these side effects.
